DECLARATION OF KASEY C. TOWNSEND IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO CROSS-DEFENDANT EDWARD D. RODRIGUEZS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 62225115) FILED BY CROSS COMPLAINANT LAU, DANIEL Y-T HWANG, LYDIA S. ALFARO, WILFREDO
ORDER GRANTING LEAVE TO FILE PROPOSED "DOE" AMENDMENTS AND CROSS-COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT EDWARD RODRIGUEZ'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINTS FILED BY DANIEL LAU, LYDIA HWANG AND WILFREDO ALFARO IS DENIED. MR. RODRIGUEZ FAILS TO MAINTAIN HIS INITIAL SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN AND, EVEN IF HE DID, THERE IS A TRIABLE DISPUTE WHETHER HE IS LIABLE AS AN EMPLOYER OF MR. ALFARO AND/OR PLAINTIFF IMER PALENCIA. "WHEN AN EMPLOYEE OF A CONTRACTOR IS INJURED, AND THE CONTRACTOR IS UNLICENSED AND UNINSURED AT THE TIME OF INJURY, THE INJURED EMPLOYEE'S RECOURSE MAY BE AGAINST NOT ONLY THE CONTRACTOR, BUT ALSO AGAINST THE LANDOWNER WHO HIRED THE CONTRACTOR, AS AN ADDITIONAL EMPLOYER." (VEBR V. CULP (2015) 241 CAL.APP.4TH 1044, 1051-1052.) MR. RODRIGUEZ HAS FAILED TO PRESENT SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SHOW THAT HE IS NOT LIABLE AS AN EMPLOYER. MOREOVER, THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT MR. RODRIGUEZ AGREED TO PAY FOR HALF OF THE COST OF THE TREE-TRIMMING WORK. (RODRIGUEZ DEC., PAR. 10.) THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER MR. RODRIGUEZ WAS ACTING AS THE EMPLOYER OF MR. ALFARO AND/OR MR. PALENCIA. THE FACT THAT MR. YANG SELECTED MR. ALFARO AND WAS THE ONLY PERSON WHO COMMUNICATED WITH HIM IS NOT DISPOSITIVE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK
ANSWER TO COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100046340) FILED BY DEFENDANT RODRIGUEZ, EDWARD D.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jean Teng, Esq. 415-524-4300 of Murchison & Cumming for Defendant Lau et al.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DANIEL LAU, LYDIA HWANG, WILFREDO ALFARO, RAYMOND YANG's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement.

Defendants' motion for determination of good faith settlement is GRANTED. No opposition filed.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-30-2017 Set For Trial On Aug-27-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-17-2018 Continued To Dec-10-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-24-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Kasey C. Townsend of Murchison & Cumming LLP, tel. No. 1-415-524-4305
appearing for the Cross-Complainants;
Atty. Stephane Davin of Rankin, Stock & Oneal, Tel. No. 1-408-293-0463
appearing for the Cross-Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Cross-defendant Edward Rodriguez's motion for summary judgment as to the cross-complaints filed by Daniel Lau, Lydia Hwang and Wilfredo Alfaro is denied. Mr. Rodriguez fails to maintain his initial summary judgment burden and, even if he did, there is a triable dispute whether he is liable as an employer of Mr. Alfaro and/or plaintiff Imer Palencia. "When an employee of a contractor is injured, and the contractor is unlicensed and uninsured at the time of injury, the injured employee's recourse may be against not only the contractor, but also against the landowner who hired the contractor, as an additional employer." (Vebr v. Culp (2015) 241 Cal.App.4th 1044, 1051-1052.) Mr. Rodriguez has failed to present sufficient facts to show that he is not liable as an employer. Moreover, the undisputed evidence shows that Mr. Rodriguez agreed to pay for half of the cost of the tree-trimming work. (Rodriguez Dec., par. 10.) There is a triable issue whether Mr. Rodriguez was acting as the employer of Mr. Alfaro and/or Mr. Palencia. The fact that Mr. Yang selected Mr. Alfaro and was the only person who communicated with him is not dispositive. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk
